translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There was a man in the land of Uz whose name was Job; and this man was perfect and upright, and one that fearedGod and abstained from evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there were born to him seven sons and three daughters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his substance was seven thousand sheep, and three thousand camels, and five hundred yoke of oxen, and five hundred she-asses, and very many servants; and this man was greater than all the children of the east.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his sons went and made a feast in the house of each one on his day; and they sent and invited their three sisters to eat and to drink with them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was so, when the days of the feasting were gone about, that Job sent and hallowed them; and he rose up early in the morning, and offered up burnt-offerings [according to] the number of them all; for Job said, It may be that my children have sinned, and cursedGod in their hearts. Thus did Job continually.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was a day when the sons ofGod came to present themselves before Jehovah; and Satan came also among them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Satan, Whence comest thou? And Satan answered Jehovah and said, From going to and fro in the earth, and from walking up and down in it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Satan, Hast thou considered my servant Job, that there is none like him on the earth, a perfect and an upright man, one that fearethGod and abstaineth from evil?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Satan answered Jehovah and said, Doth Job fearGod for nought?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hast not thou made a hedge about him, and about his house, and about all that he hath on every side? Thou hast blessed the work of his hands, and his substance is spread abroad in the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But put forth thy hand now and touch all that he hath, [and see] if he will not curse thee to thy face!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Satan, Behold, all that he hath is in thy hand; only upon himself put not forth thy hand. So Satan went forth from the presence of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was a day when his sons and his daughters were eating and drinking wine in the house of their brother, the firstborn.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there came a messenger to Job and said, The oxen were ploughing, and the asses feeding beside them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and [they of] Sheba fell [upon them] and took them, and the servants have they smitten with the edge of the sword; and I only am escaped, alone, to tell thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: While he was yet speaking, there came another and said, The fire ofGod fell from heaven and burned up the sheep and the servants, and consumed them; and I only am escaped, alone, to tell thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: While he was yet speaking, there came another and said, The Chaldeans made three bands, and fell upon the camels and took them, and the servants have they smitten with the edge of the sword; and I only am escaped, alone, to tell thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: While he was yet speaking, there came another and said, Thy sons and thy daughters were eating and drinking wine in the house of their brother, the firstborn;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and behold, there came a great wind from over the wilderness, and smote the four corners of the house, and it fell upon the young men, and they died; and I only am escaped, alone, to tell thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Job rose up, and rent his mantle, and shaved his head, and fell down on the ground, and worshipped;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he said, Naked came I out of my mother's womb, and naked shall I return thither: Jehovah gave, and Jehovah hath taken away; blessed be the name of Jehovah!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In all this Job sinned not, nor ascribed anything unseemly toGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was a day when the sons ofGod came to present themselves before Jehovah, and Satan also came among them to present himself before Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Satan, From whence comest thou? And Satan answered Jehovah and said, From going to and fro in the earth, and from walking up and down in it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Satan, Hast thou considered my servant Job, that there is none like him on the earth, a perfect and an upright man, one that fearethGod and abstaineth from evil? and still he remaineth firm in his integrity, though thou movedst me against him, to swallow him up without cause.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Satan answered Jehovah and said, Skin for skin, yea, all that a man hath will he give for his life;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but put forth thy hand now, and touch his bone and his flesh, [and see] if he will not curse thee to thy face!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Satan, Behold, he is in thy hand; only spare his life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Satan went forth from the presence of Jehovah; and he smote Job with a grievous botch from the sole of his foot unto his crown.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he took a potsherd to scrape himself with; and he sat among the ashes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his wife said to him, Dost thou still remain firm in thine integrity? curseGod and die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he said to her, Thou speakest as one of the foolish women speaketh. We have also received good fromGod, and should we not receive evil? In all this Job did not sin with his lips.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And three friends of Job heard of all this evil that was come upon him. And they came each one from his place: Eliphaz the Temanite, and Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite; and they made an appointment together to come to condole with him and to comfort him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they lifted up their eyes afar off, and knew him not, they lifted up their voice and wept. And they rent every one his mantle, and sprinkled dust upon their heads toward the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they sat down with him on the ground seven days and seven nights; and none spoke a word to him; for they saw that [his] anguish was very great.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: After this, Job opened his mouth and cursed his day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Job answered and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let the day perish in which I was born, and the night that said, There is a man child conceived.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That day — let it be darkness, let notGod care for it from above, neither let light shine upon it:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let darkness and the shadow of death claim it; let clouds dwell upon it; let darkeners of the day terrify it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That night — let gloom seize upon it; let it not rejoice among the days of the year; let it not come into the number of the months.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, let that night be barren; let no joyful sound come therein;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let them curse it that curse the day, who are ready to rouse Leviathan;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let the stars of its twilight be dark; let it wait for light, and have none, neither let it see the eyelids of the dawn:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because it shut not up the doors of the womb that bore me, and hid not trouble from mine eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore did I not die from the womb, — come forth from the belly and expire?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why did the knees meet me? and wherefore the breasts, that I should suck?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For now should I have lain down and been quiet; I should have slept: then had I been at rest,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: With kings and counsellors of the earth, who build desolate places for themselves,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Or with princes who had gold, who filled their houses with silver;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Or as a hidden untimely birth I had not been; as infants that have not seen the light.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There the wicked cease from troubling; and there the wearied are at rest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The prisoners together are at ease; they hear not the voice of the taskmaster.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The small and great are there, and the bondman freed from his master.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore is light given to him that is in trouble, and life to those bitter of soul,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who long for death, and it [cometh] not, and dig for it more than for hidden treasures;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who rejoice even exultingly and are glad when they find the grave? —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To the man whose way is hidden, and whomGod hath hedged in?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For my sighing cometh before my bread, and my groanings are poured out like the waters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I feared a fear, and it hath come upon me, and that which I dreaded hath come to me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I was not in safety, neither had I quietness, neither was I at rest, and trouble came.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If a word were essayed to thee, wouldest thou be grieved? But who can refrain from speaking?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, thou hast instructed many, and thou hast strengthened the weak hands;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy words have upholden him that was stumbling, and thou hast braced up the bending knees:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But now it is come upon thee, and thou grievest; it toucheth thee, and thou art troubled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hath not thy piety been thy confidence, and the perfection of thy ways thy hope?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember, I pray thee, who that was innocent has perished? and where were the upright cut off?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Even as I have seen, they that plough iniquity and sow mischief, reap the same.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By the breath ofGod they perish, and by the blast of his nostrils are they consumed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The roar of the lion, and the voice of the fierce lion, and the teeth of the young lions, are broken;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The old lion perisheth for lack of prey, and the whelps of the lioness are scattered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now to me a word was secretly brought, and mine ear received a whisper thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In thoughts from visions of the night, when deep sleep falleth on men: —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Fear came on me, and trembling, and made all my bones to shake;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a spirit passed before my face — the hair of my flesh stood up —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It stood still; I could not discern the appearance thereof: a form was before mine eyes; I heard a slight murmur and a voice:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall [mortal] man be more just thanGod? Shall a man be purer than his Maker?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lo, he trusteth not his servants, and his angels he chargeth with folly:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How much more them that dwell in houses of clay, whose foundation is in the dust, who are crushed as the moth!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From morning to evening are they smitten: without any heeding it, they perish for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is not their tent-cord torn away in them? they die, and without wisdom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Call, I pray thee! Is there any that answereth thee? and to which of the holy ones wilt thou turn?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For vexation killeth the foolish man, and envy slayeth the simple.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I myself saw the foolish taking root, but suddenly I cursed his habitation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His children are far from safety, and they are crushed in the gate, and there is no deliverer:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whose harvest the hungry eateth up, and taketh even out of the thorns; and the snare gapeth for his substance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For evil cometh not forth from the dust, neither doth trouble spring out of the ground;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For man is born to trouble, as the sparks fly upwards.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as for me I will seek untoGod, and untoGod commit my cause;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who doeth great things and unsearchable, marvellous things without number;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who giveth rain on the face of the earth, and sendeth waters on the face of the fields;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Setting up on high those that are low; and mourners are exalted to prosperity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He disappointeth the devices of the crafty, and their hands carry not out the enterprise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He taketh the wise in their own craftiness; and the counsel of the wily is carried headlong:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They meet with darkness in the daytime, and grope at midday as in the night.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he saveth the needy from the sword, from their mouth, and from the hand of the mighty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So the poor hath what he hopeth for, and unrighteousness stoppeth her mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, happy is the man whomGod correcteth; therefore despise not the chastening of the Almighty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he maketh sore, and bindeth up; he woundeth, and his hands make whole.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He will deliver thee in six troubles, and in seven there shall no evil touch thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In famine he will redeem thee from death, and in war from the power of the sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt be hidden from the scourge of the tongue; and thou shalt not be afraid of destruction when it cometh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At destruction and famine thou shalt laugh, and of the beasts of the earth thou shalt not be afraid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou shalt be in league with the stones of the field, and the beasts of the field shall be at peace with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt know that thy tent is in peace; and thou wilt survey thy fold, and miss nothing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt know that thy seed is numerous, and thine offspring as the herb of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt come to the grave in a ripe age, as a shock of corn is brought in in its season.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold this, we have searched it out, so it is; hear it, and know thou it for thyself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Job answered and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Oh that my grief were thoroughly weighed, and all my calamity laid in the balances!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For now it would be heavier than the sand of the seas; therefore my words are vehement.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the arrows of the Almighty are within me, their poison drinketh up my spirit: the terrors ofGod are arrayed against me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Doth the wild ass bray by the grass? loweth an ox over his fodder?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall that which is insipid be eaten without salt? Is there any taste in the white of an egg?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What my soul refuseth to touch, that is as my loathsome food.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Oh that I might have my request, and thatGod would grant my desire!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And that it would pleaseGod to crush me, that he would let loose his hand and cut me off!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then should I yet have comfort; and in the pain which spareth not I would rejoice that I have not denied the words of the Holy One.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What is my strength, that I should hope? and what is mine end, that I should have patience?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is my strength the strength of stones? is my flesh of brass?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is it not that there is no help in me, and soundness is driven away from me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For him that is fainting kindness [is meet] from his friend; or he forsaketh the fear of the Almighty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My brethren have dealt deceitfully as a stream, as the channel of streams which pass away,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Which are turbid by reason of the ice, in which the snow hideth itself:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At the time they diminish, they are dried up; when heat affecteth them, they vanish from their place:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They wind about in the paths of their course, they go off into the waste and perish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The caravans of Tema looked, the companies of Sheba counted on them:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They are ashamed at their hope; they come thither, and are confounded.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So now ye are nothing; ye see a terrible object and are afraid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Did I say, Bring unto me, and make me a present from your substance?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Or, rescue me from the hand of the oppressor, and redeem me from the hand of the violent?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Teach me, and I will hold my tongue; and cause me to understand wherein I have erred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How forcible are right words! but what doth your upbraiding reprove?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do ye imagine to reprove words? The speeches of one that is desperate are indeed for the wind.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, ye overwhelm the fatherless, and dig [a pit] for your friend.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now therefore if ye will, look upon me; and it shall be to your face if I lie.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Return, I pray you, let there be no wrong; yea, return again, my righteousness shall be in it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is there wrong in my tongue? cannot my taste discern mischievous things?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hath not man a life of labour upon earth? and are not his days like the days of a hireling?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As a bondman earnestly desireth the shadow, and a hireling expecteth his wages,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So am I made to possess months of vanity, and wearisome nights are appointed to me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I lie down, I say, When shall I rise up, and the darkness be gone? and I am full of tossings until the dawn.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My flesh is clothed with worms and clods of dust; my skin is broken, and suppurates.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My days are swifter than a weaver's shuttle, and are spent without hope.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember thou that my life is wind; mine eye shall no more see good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The eye of him that hath seen me shall behold me no [more]: thine eyes are upon me, and I am not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The cloud consumeth and vanisheth away; so he that goeth down to Sheol shall not come up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He shall return no more to his house, neither shall his place know him again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore I will not restrain my mouth: I will speak in the anguish of my spirit; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Am I a sea, or a sea-monster, that thou settest a watch over me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When I say, My bed shall comfort me, my couch shall ease my complaint;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then thou scarest me with dreams, and terrifiest me through visions;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So that my soul chooseth strangling, death, rather than my bones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I loathe it; I shall not live always: let me alone, for my days are a breath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What is man, that thou makest much of him? and that thou settest thy heart upon him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And that thou visitest him every morning, triest him every moment?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How long wilt thou not look away from me, nor let me alone till I swallow down my spittle?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Have I sinned, what do I unto thee, thou Observer of men? Why hast thou set me as an object of assault for thee, so that I am become a burden to myself?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And why dost not thou forgive my transgression and take away mine iniquity? for now shall I lie down in the dust, and thou shalt seek me early, and I shall not be.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Bildad the Shuhite answered and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How long wilt thou speak these things? and the words of thy mouth be a strong wind?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: DothGod pervert judgment, and the Almighty pervert justice?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thy children have sinned against him, he hath also given them over into the hand of their transgression.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thou seek earnestly untoGod, and make thy supplication to the Almighty,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thou be pure and upright, surely now he will awake for thee, and make the habitation of thy righteousness prosperous;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And though thy beginning was small, yet thine end shall be very great.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For inquire, I pray thee, of the former generation, and attend to the researches of their fathers;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we are [but] of yesterday, and know nothing, for our days upon earth are a shadow.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall not they teach thee, [and] tell thee, and utter words out of their heart?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Doth the papyrus shoot up without mire? doth the reed-grass grow without water?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whilst it is yet in its greenness [and] not cut down, it withereth before any [other] grass.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So are the paths of all that forgetGod; and the profane man's hope shall perish,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whose confidence shall be cut off, and his reliance is a spider's web.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He shall lean upon his house, and it shall not stand; he shall lay hold on it, but it shall not endure.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He is full of sap before the sun, and his sprout shooteth forth over his garden;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His roots are entwined about the stoneheap; he seeth the place of stones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If he destroy him from his place, then it shall deny him: I have not seen thee!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, this is the joy of his way, and out of the dust shall others grow.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold,God will not cast off a perfect man, neither will he take evil-doers by the hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whilst he would fill thy mouth with laughing and thy lips with shouting,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They that hate thee shall be clothed with shame, and the tent of the wicked be no more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Job answered and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of a truth I know it is so; but how can man be just withGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If he shall choose to strive with him, he cannot answer him one thing of a thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He is wise in heart and mighty in strength: who hath hardened himself against him, and had peace?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who removeth mountains, and they know it not, when he overturneth them in his anger;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who shaketh the earth out of its place, and the pillars thereof tremble;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who commandeth the sun, and it riseth not, and he sealeth up the stars;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who alone spreadeth out the heavens, and treadeth upon the high waves of the sea;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who maketh the Bear, Orion, and the Pleiades, and the chambers of the south;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who doeth great things past finding out, and wonders without number.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lo, he goeth by me, and I see [him] not; and he passeth along, and I perceive him not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, he taketh away: who will hinder him? Who will say unto him, What doest thou?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God withdraweth not his anger; the proud helpers stoop under him:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How much less shall I answer him, choose out my words [to strive] with him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whom, though I were righteous, [yet] would I not answer; I would make supplication to my judge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I had called, and he had answered me, I would not believe that he hearkened to my voice, —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He, who crusheth me with a tempest, and multiplieth my wounds without cause.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He suffereth me not to take my breath, for he filleth me with bitternesses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be it a question of strength, lo, [he is] strong; and be it of judgment, who will set me a time?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I justified myself, mine own mouth would condemn me; were I perfect, he would prove me perverse.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Were I perfect, [yet] would I not know my soul: I would despise my life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is all one; therefore I said, he destroyeth the perfect and the wicked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If the scourge kill suddenly, he mocketh at the trial of the innocent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The earth is given over into the hand of the wicked [man]; he covereth the faces of its judges. If not, who then is it?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And my days are swifter than a runner: they flee away, they see no good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They pass by like skiffs of reed; as an eagle that swoops upon the prey.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I say, I will forget my complaint, I will leave off my [sad] countenance, and brighten up,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am afraid of all my sorrows; I know that thou wilt not hold me innocent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be it that I am wicked, why then do I labour in vain?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I washed myself with snow-water, and cleansed my hands in purity,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then wouldest thou plunge me in the ditch, and mine own clothes would abhor me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he is not a man, as I am, that I should answer him; that we should come together in judgment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is not an umpire between us, who should lay his hand upon us both.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let him take his rod away from me, and let not his terror make me afraid,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Then] I will speak, and not fear him; but it is not so with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My soul is weary of my life: I will give free course to my complaint; I will speak in the bitterness of my soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will say untoGod, Do not condemn me; shew me wherefore thou strivest with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Doth it please thee to oppress, that thou shouldest despise the work of thy hands, and shine upon the counsel of the wicked?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hast thou eyes of flesh? or seest thou as man seeth?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Are thy days as the days of a mortal? are thy years as a man's days,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That thou searchest after mine iniquity, and inquirest into my sin;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Since thou knowest that I am not wicked, and that there is none that delivereth out of thy hand?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy hands have bound me together and made me as one, round about; yet dost thou swallow me up!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember, I beseech thee, that thou hast made me as clay, and wilt bring me into dust again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hast thou not poured me out as milk, and curdled me like cheese?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast clothed me with skin and flesh, and knit me together with bones and sinews;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast granted me life and favour, and thy care hath preserved my spirit;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these things didst thou hide in thy heart; I know that this was with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I sinned, thou wouldest mark me, and thou wouldest not acquit me of mine iniquity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I were wicked, woe unto me! and righteous, I will not lift up my head, being [so] full of shame, and beholding mine affliction; —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it increaseth: thou huntest me as a fierce lion; and ever again thou shewest thy marvellous power upon me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou renewest thy witnesses before me and increasest thy displeasure against me; successions [of evil] and a time of toil are with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And wherefore didst thou bring me forth out of the womb? I had expired, and no eye had seen me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I should be as though I had not been; I should have been carried from the womb to the grave.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Are not my days few? cease then and let me alone, that I may revive a little,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Before I go, and never to return, — to the land of darkness and the shadow of death;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A land of gloom, as darkness itself; of the shadow of death, without any order, where the light is as thick darkness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Zophar the Naamathite answered and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Should not the multitude of words be answered? and should a man of much talk be justified?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Should thy fictions make men hold their peace? and shouldest thou mock, and no one make [thee] ashamed?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou sayest, My doctrine is pure, and I am clean in thine eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But oh thatGod would speak, and open his lips against thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And that he would shew thee the secrets of wisdom, how that they are the double of what is realised; and know thatGod passeth by [much] of thine iniquity!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Canst thou by searching find outGod? canst thou find out the Almighty to perfection?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [It is as] the heights of heaven; what wilt thou do? deeper than Sheol; what canst thou know?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The measure thereof is longer than the earth, and broader than the sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If he pass by, and shut up, and call to judgment, who can hinder him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he knoweth vain men, and seeth wickedness when [man] doth not consider it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yet a senseless man will make bold, though man be born [like] the foal of a wild ass.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thou prepare thy heart and stretch out thy hands toward him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thou put far away the iniquity which is in thy hand, and let not wrong dwell in thy tents;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Surely then shalt thou lift up thy face without spot, and thou shalt be stedfast and shalt not fear:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou shalt forget misery; as waters that are passed away shalt thou remember it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And life shall arise brighter than noonday; though thou be enshrouded in darkness, thou shalt be as the morning,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt have confidence, because there shall be hope; and having searched about [thee], thou shalt take rest in safety.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, thou shalt lie down, and none shall make thee afraid; and many shall seek thy favour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the eyes of the wicked shall fail, and [all] refuge shall vanish from them, and their hope [shall be] the breathing out of life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Job answered and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Truly ye are the people, and wisdom shall die with you!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I also have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you; and who knoweth not such things as these?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am to be one that is a derision to his friend, I who call uponGod, and whom he will answer: a derision is the just upright [man].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that is ready to stumble with the foot is a lamp despised in the thought of him that is at ease.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The tents of desolators are in peace, and they that provokeGod are secure; into whose handGod bringeth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But ask now the beasts, and they shall teach thee; and the fowl of the heavens, and they shall tell thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Or speak to the earth, and it shall teach thee; and the fishes of the sea shall declare unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who knoweth not in all these, that the hand of Jehovah hath wrought this?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In whose hand is the soul of every living thing, and the spirit of all flesh of man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Doth not the ear try words, as the palate tasteth food?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: With the aged is wisdom, and in length of days understanding.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: With him is wisdom and might; he hath counsel and understanding.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, he breaketh down, and it is not built again; he shutteth up a man, and there is no opening.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, he withholdeth the waters, and they dry up; and he sendeth them out, and they overturn the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: With him is strength and effectual knowledge; the deceived and the deceiver are his.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He leadeth counsellors away spoiled, and judges maketh he fools;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He weakeneth the government of kings, and bindeth their loins with a fetter;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He leadeth priests away spoiled, and overthroweth the mighty;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He depriveth of speech the trusty, and taketh away the judgment of the elders;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He poureth contempt upon nobles, and slackeneth the girdle of the mighty;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He discovereth deep things out of darkness, and bringeth out into light the shadow of death;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He increaseth the nations, and destroyeth them; he spreadeth out the nations, and bringeth them in;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He taketh away the understanding of the chiefs of the people of the earth, and causeth them to wander in a pathless waste.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They grope in the dark without light, and he maketh them to stagger like a drunkard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lo, mine eye hath seen all [this], mine ear hath heard and understood it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What ye know, I know also: I am not inferior to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I will speak to the Almighty, and will find pleasure in reasoning withGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For ye indeed are forgers of lies, ye are all physicians of no value.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Oh that ye would be altogether silent! and it would be your wisdom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear now my defence, and hearken to the pleadings of my lips.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Will ye speak unrighteously forGod? and for him speak deceit?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Will ye accept his person? will ye contend forGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Will it be well if he should search you out? or as one mocketh at a man, will ye mock at him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He will certainly reprove you, if ye do secretly accept persons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall not his excellency terrify you? and his dread fall upon you?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Your memorable sayings are proverbs of ashes, your bulwarks are bulwarks of mire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hold your peace from me, and I will speak, and let come on me what [will]!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore should I take my flesh in my teeth, and put my life in my hand?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, if he slay me, yet would I trust in him; but I will defend mine own ways before him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This also shall be my salvation, that a profane man shall not come before his face.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear attentively my speech and my declaration with your ears.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold now, I have ordered the cause; I know that I shall be justified.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who is he that contendeth with me? For if I were silent now, I should expire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Only do not two things unto me; then will I not hide myself from thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Withdraw thy hand far from me; and let not thy terror make me afraid:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then call, and I will answer; or I will speak, and answer thou me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How many are mine iniquities and sins? Make me to know my transgression and my sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore dost thou hide thy face, and countest me for thine enemy?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wilt thou terrify a driven leaf? and wilt thou pursue dry stubble?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou writest bitter things against me, and makest me to possess the iniquities of my youth;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou puttest my feet in the stocks, and markest all my paths; thou settest a bound about the soles of my feet; —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: One who, as a rotten thing consumeth, as a garment that the moth eateth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Man, born of woman, is of few days, and full of trouble.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He cometh forth like a flower, and is cut down; and he fleeth as a shadow, and continueth not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yet dost thou open thine eyes upon such a one, and bringest me into judgment with thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who can bring a clean [man] out of the unclean? Not one!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If his days are determined, if the number of his months is with thee, [and] thou hast appointed his bounds which he must not pass,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Look away from him; and let him rest, till he accomplish, as a hireling, his day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For there is hope for a tree: if it be cut down, it will sprout again, and its tender branch will not cease;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Though its root grow old in the earth, and its stock die in the ground,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yet through the scent of water it will bud, and put forth boughs like a young plant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But a man dieth, and is prostrate; yea, man expireth, and where is he?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The waters recede from the lake, and the river wasteth and drieth up:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So man lieth down, and riseth not again; till the heavens be no more, they do not awake, nor are raised out of their sleep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Oh that thou wouldest hide me in Sheol, that thou wouldest keep me secret until thine anger be past, that thou wouldest appoint me a set time, and remember me, —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (If a man die, shall he live [again]?) all the days of my time of toil would I wait, till my change should come:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou wouldest call, and I would answer thee; thou wouldest have a desire after the work of thy hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For now thou numberest my steps: dost thou not watch over my sin?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My transgression is sealed up in a bag, and thou heapest up mine iniquity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And indeed a mountain falling cometh to nought, and the rock is removed out of its place;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The waters wear the stones, the floods thereof wash away the dust of the earth; and thou destroyest the hope of man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou prevailest for ever against him, and he passeth away; thou changest his countenance, and dismissest him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His sons come to honour, and he knoweth it not; and they are brought low, and he perceiveth it not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But his flesh hath pain for himself alone, and his soul mourneth for himself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Should a wise man answer with windy knowledge, and fill his belly with the east wind,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Reasoning with unprofitable talk, and with speeches which do no good?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, thou makest piety of none effect, and restrainest meditation beforeGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thy mouth uttereth thine iniquity, and thou hast chosen the tongue of the crafty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thine own mouth condemneth thee, and not I; and thy lips testify against thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Art thou the first man that was born? and wast thou brought forth before the hills?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hast thou listened in the secret council ofGod? And hast thou absorbed wisdom for thyself?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What knowest thou that we know not? [what] understandest thou which is not in us?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Both the greyheaded and the aged are with us, older than thy father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Are the consolations ofGod too small for thee? and the word gently spoken to thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why doth thy heart carry thee away? and why do thine eyes wink?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That thou turnest thy spirit againstGod, and lettest words go out of thy mouth?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What is man, that he should be pure? and he that is born of a woman, that he should be righteous?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, he putteth no trust in his holy ones, and the heavens are not pure in his sight:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How much less the abominable and corrupt, — man, that drinketh unrighteousness like water!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will shew thee, listen to me; and what I have seen I will declare;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Which wise men have told from their fathers, and have not hidden;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Unto whom alone the earth was given, and no stranger passed among them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All his days the wicked man is tormented, and numbered years are allotted to the violent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sound of terrors is in his ears: in prosperity the destroyer cometh upon him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He believeth not that he shall return out of darkness, and he is singled out for the sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He wandereth abroad for bread, — where may it be? He knoweth that the day of darkness is ready at his hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Distress and anguish make him afraid; they prevail against him, as a king ready for the battle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he hath stretched out his hand againstGod, and strengthened himself against the Almighty:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He runneth against him, with [outstretched] neck, with the thick bosses of his bucklers;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he hath covered his face with his fatness, and gathered fat upon [his] flanks.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he dwelleth in desolate cities, in houses that no man inhabiteth, which are destined to become heaps.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He shall not become rich, neither shall his substance continue, and their possessions shall not extend upon the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He shall not depart out of darkness; the flame shall dry up his branches; and by the breath of his mouth shall he go away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let him not trust in vanity: he is deceived, for vanity shall be his recompense;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It shall be complete before his day, and his branch shall not be green.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He shall shake off his unripe grapes as a vine, and shall cast his flower as an olive.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the family of the ungodly shall be barren, and fire shall consume the tents of bribery.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They conceive mischief, and bring forth iniquity, and their belly prepareth deceit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Job answered and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have heard many such things: grievous comforters are ye all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall words of wind have an end? or what provoketh thee that thou answerest?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I also could speak as ye: if your soul were in my soul's stead, I could join together words against you, and shake my head at you;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [But] I would encourage you with my mouth, and the solace of my lips should assuage [your pain].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I speak, my pain is not assuaged; and if I forbear, what am I eased?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But now he hath made me weary; ...thou hast made desolate all my family;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast shrivelled me up! it is become a witness; and my leanness riseth up against me, it beareth witness to my face.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His anger teareth and pursueth me; he gnasheth with his teeth against me; [as] mine adversary he sharpeneth his eyes at me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They gape upon me with their mouth; they smite my cheeks reproachfully; they range themselves together against me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God hath delivered me over to the iniquitous man, and hurled me into the hands of the wicked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I was at rest, but he hath shattered me; he hath taken me by the neck and shaken me to pieces, and set me up for his mark.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His arrows encompass me round about, he cleaveth my reins asunder and doth not spare; he poureth out my gall upon the ground.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He breaketh me with breach upon breach; he runneth upon me like a mighty man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have sewed sackcloth upon my skin, and rolled my horn in the dust.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My face is red with weeping, and on my eyelids is the shadow of death;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Although there is no violence in my hands, and my prayer is pure.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: O earth, cover not my blood, and let there be no place for my cry!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Even now, behold, my Witness is in the heavens, and he that voucheth for me is in the heights.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My friends are my mockers; mine eye poureth out tears untoGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Oh that there were arbitration for a man withGod, as a son of man for his friend!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For years [few] in number shall pass, — and I shall go the way [whence] I shall not return.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My breath is corrupt, my days are extinct, the graves are mine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Are there not mockers around me? and doth [not] mine eye abide in their provocation?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lay down now [a pledge], be thou surety for me with thyself: who is he that striketh hands with me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou hast hidden their heart from understanding; therefore thou wilt not exalt [them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that betrayeth friends for a prey — even the eyes of his children shall fail.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he hath made me a proverb of the peoples; and I am become one to be spit on in the face.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And mine eye is dim by reason of grief, and all my members are as a shadow.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Upright men [shall be] astonished at this, and the innocent shall be stirred up against the ungodly;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the righteous shall hold on his way, and he that hath clean hands shall increase in strength.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as for you all, pray come on again; and I shall not find one wise man among you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My days are past, my purposes are broken off, the cherished thoughts of my heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They change the night into day; the light [they imagine] near in presence of the darkness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I wait, Sheol is my house; I spread my bed in the darkness:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I cry to the grave, Thou art my father! to the worm, My mother, and my sister!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And where is then my hope? yea, my hope, who shall see it?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It shall go down to the bars of Sheol, when [our] rest shall be together in the dust.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Bildad the Shuhite answered and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How long will ye hunt for words? Be intelligent, and then we will speak.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore are we counted as beasts, and reputed stupid in your sight?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou that tearest thyself in thine anger, shall the earth be forsaken for thee? and shall the rock be removed out of its place?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, the light of the wicked shall be put out, and the flame of his fire shall not shine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The light shall become dark in his tent, and his lamp over him shall be put out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The steps of his strength shall be straitened, and his own counsel shall cast him down.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he is sent into the net by his own feet, and he walketh on the meshes;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The gin taketh [him] by the heel, the snare layeth hold on him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: A cord is hidden for him in the ground, and his trap in the way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Terrors make him afraid on every side, and chase him at his footsteps.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His strength is hunger-bitten, and calamity is ready at his side.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The firstborn of death devoureth the members of his body; it will devour his members.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His confidence shall be rooted out of his tent, and it shall lead him away to the king of terrors:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They who are none of his shall dwell in his tent; brimstone shall be showered upon his habitation:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His roots shall be dried up beneath, and above shall his branch be cut off;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His remembrance shall perish from the earth, and he shall have no name on the pasture-grounds.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He is driven from light into darkness, and chased out of the world.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath neither son nor grandson among his people, nor any remaining in the places of his sojourn.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They that come after shall be astonished at his day, as they that went before [them] were affrighted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Surely, such are the dwellings of the unrighteous man, and such the place of him that knoweth notGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Job answered and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How long will ye vex my soul, and crush me with words?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These ten times have ye reproached me; ye are not ashamed to stupefy me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And be it [that] I have erred, mine error remaineth with myself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If indeed ye will magnify yourselves against me, and prove against me my reproach,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Know now thatGod hath overthrown me, and hath surrounded me with his net.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I cry out of wrong, and I am not heard; I cry aloud, but there is no judgment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath hedged up my way that I cannot pass, and he hath set darkness in my paths.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath stripped me of my glory, and taken the crown from my head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He breaketh me down on every side, and I am gone; and my hope hath he torn up as a tree.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he hath kindled his anger against me, and hath counted me unto him as one of his enemies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His troops have come together and cast up their way against me, and have encamped round about my tent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath put my brethren far from me, and mine acquaintance are quite estranged from me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My kinsfolk have failed, and my known friends have forgotten me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sojourners in my house and my maids count me as a stranger; I am an alien in their sight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I called my servant, and he answered not; I entreated him with my mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My breath is strange to my wife, and my entreaties to the children of my [mother's] womb.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Even young children despise me; I rise up, and they speak against me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All my intimate friends abhor me, and they whom I loved are turned against me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My bones cleave to my skin and to my flesh, and I am escaped with the skin of my teeth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Have pity upon me, have pity upon me, ye my friends; for the hand ofGod hath touched me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why do ye persecute me asGod, and are not satisfied with my flesh?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Oh would that my words were written! oh that they were inscribed in a book!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That with an iron style and lead they were graven in the rock for ever!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [as for] me, I know that my Redeemer liveth, and the Last, he shall stand upon the earth;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [if] after my skin this shall be destroyed, yet from out of my flesh shall I seeGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whom I shall see for myself, and mine eyes shall behold, and not another: — my reins are consumed within me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If ye say, How shall we persecute him? when the root of the matter is found in me,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be ye yourselves afraid of the sword! for the sword is fury against misdeeds, that ye may know there is a judgment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Zophar the Naamathite answered and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore do my thoughts give me an answer, and for this is my haste within me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I hear a reproof putting me to shame; and [my] spirit answereth me by mine understanding.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Knowest thou [not] this, that of old, since man was placed upon earth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The exultation of the wicked is short, and the joy of the ungodly man but for a moment?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Though his height mount up to the heavens, and his head reach unto the clouds,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Like his own dung doth he perish for ever; they which have seen him shall say, Where is he?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He flieth away as a dream, and is not found; and is chased away as a vision of the night.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The eye which saw him shall [see him] not again; and his place beholdeth him no more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His children shall seek the favour of the poor, and his hands restore his wealth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His bones were full of his youthful strength; but it shall lie down with him in the dust.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Though wickedness be sweet in his mouth [and] he hide it under his tongue,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Though] he spare it, and forsake it not, but keep it within his mouth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His food is turned in his bowels; it is the gall of asps within him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath swallowed down riches, but he shall vomit them up again:God shall cast them out of his belly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He shall suck the poison of asps; the viper's tongue shall kill him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He shall not see streams, rivers, brooks of honey and butter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That which he laboured for shall he restore, and not swallow down; its restitution shall be according to the value, and he shall not rejoice [therein].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he hath oppressed, hath forsaken the poor; he hath violently taken away a house that he did not build.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because he knew no rest in his craving, he shall save nought of what he most desired.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Nothing escaped his greediness; therefore his prosperity shall not endure.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the fulness of his sufficiency he shall be in straits; every hand of the wretched shall come upon him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It shall be that, to fill his belly, he will cast his fierce anger upon him, and will rain it upon him into his flesh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If he have fled from the iron weapon, the bow of brass shall strike him through.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He draweth it forth; it cometh out of his body, and the glittering point out of his gall: terrors are upon him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All darkness is laid up for his treasures: a fire not blown shall devour him; it shall feed upon what is left in his tent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The heavens shall reveal his iniquity, and the earth shall rise up against him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The increase of his house shall depart, flowing away in the day of his anger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is the portion of the wicked man fromGod, and the heritage appointed to him byGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Job answered and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear attentively my speech, and let this replace your consolations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Suffer me and I will speak; and after I have spoken, mock on!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As for me, is my complaint to a man? or wherefore should not my spirit be impatient?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Mark me, and be astonished, and lay the hand upon the mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Even when I think [thereon], I am affrighted, and trembling taketh hold of my flesh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore do the wicked live, grow old, yea, become mighty in power?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their seed is established with them in their sight, and their offspring before their eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their houses are safe from fear, neither is the rod ofGod upon them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their bull gendereth, and faileth not; their cow calveth, and casteth not her calf.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They send forth their little ones like a flock, and their children dance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They shout to the tambour and harp, and rejoice at the sound of the pipe.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They spend their days in prosperity, and in a moment go down to Sheol.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they say untoGod, Depart from us, for we desire not the knowledge of thy ways!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What is the Almighty that we should serve him? and what are we profited if we pray unto him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, their prosperity is not in their hand. The counsel of the wicked be far from me!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How often is the lamp of the wicked put out, and cometh their calamity upon them? Doth he distribute sorrows [to them] in his anger?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Do they become as stubble before the wind, and as chaff that the storm carrieth away?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God layeth up [the punishment of] his iniquity for his children; he rewardeth him, and he shall know [it]:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His eyes shall see his destruction, and he shall drink of the fury of the Almighty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For what pleasure should he have in his house after him, when the number of his months is cut off?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Can any teachGod knowledge? And he it is that judgeth those that are high.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: One dieth in his full strength, being wholly at ease and quiet;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His sides are full of fat, and the marrow of his bones is moistened;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And another dieth in bitterness of soul, and hath not tasted good:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Together they lie down in the dust, and the worms cover them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lo, I know your thoughts, and the devices ye wrongfully imagine against me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For ye say, Where is the house of the noble? and where the tent of the dwellings of the wicked?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Have ye not asked the wayfarers? and do ye not regard their tokens:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That the wicked is reserved for the day of calamity? They are led forth to the day of wrath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who shall declare his way to his face? and who shall repay him what he hath done?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yet is he carried to the graves, and watch is kept over the tomb.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The clods of the valley are sweet unto him; and every man followeth suit after him, as there were innumerable before him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How then comfort ye me in vain? Your answers remain perfidious.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Can a man be profitable toGod? surely it is unto himself that the wise man is profitable.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is it any pleasure to the Almighty if thou art righteous? And is it gain [to him] that thou makest thy ways perfect?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Will he reason with thee for fear of thee? Will he enter with thee into judgment?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is not thy wickedness great? and thine iniquities without end?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou hast taken a pledge of thy brother for nought, and stripped off the clothing of the naked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast not given water to the fainting to drink, and thou hast withholden bread from the hungry.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the powerful man, he had the land; and the man of high rank dwelt in it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Widows hast thou sent empty away, and the arms of the fatherless have been broken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore snares are round about thee, and sudden fear troubleth thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Or darkness, that thou canst not see, and floods of waters cover thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is notGod in the height of the heavens? And behold the summit of the stars: how exalted are they!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou sayest, What dothGod know? will he judge through the dark cloud?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thick clouds are a covering to him, that he seeth not; and he walketh on the vault of the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Dost thou mark the ancient path which wicked men have trodden?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who were carried off before the time, whose foundation was overflowed with a flood;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who said untoGod, Depart from us! and what could the Almighty do to them?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yet he filled their houses with good. But the counsel of the wicked is far from me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The righteous see it, and are glad; and the innocent laugh them to scorn:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is not he who rose against us destroyed, and doth not the fire consume his residue?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Reconcile thyself now with him, and be at peace: thereby good shall come unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Receive, I pray thee, instruction from his mouth, and lay up his words in thy heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thou return to the Almighty, thou shalt be built up. If thou remove unrighteousness far from thy tents,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And put the precious ore with the dust, and [the gold of] Ophir among the stones of the torrents,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the Almighty will be thy precious ore, and silver heaped up unto thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, then shalt thou delight thyself in the Almighty, and shalt lift up thy face untoGod:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou shalt make thy prayer unto him, and he will hear thee, and thou shalt pay thy vows;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt decree a thing, and it shall be established unto thee; and light shall shine upon thy ways.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When they are made low, then thou shalt say, Rise up! and he shall save him that is of downcast eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Even] him that is not innocent shall he deliver; yea, he shall be delivered by the pureness of thy hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Job answered and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Even to-day is my complaint bitter: my stroke is heavier than my groaning.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Oh that I knew where I might find him, that I might come to his seat!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I would order the cause before him, and fill my mouth with arguments;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I would know the words he would answer me, and understand what he would say unto me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Would he plead against me with [his] great power? Nay; but he would give heed unto me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There would an upright man reason with him; and I should be delivered for ever from my judge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lo, I go forward, but he is not there; and backward, but I do not perceive him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On the left hand, where he doth work, but I behold [him] not; he hideth himself on the right hand, and I see [him] not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he knoweth the way that I take; he trieth me, I shall come forth as gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My foot hath held to his steps; his way have I kept, and not turned aside.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Neither have I gone back from the commandment of his lips; I have laid up the words of his mouth more than the purpose of my own heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he is in one [mind], and who can turn him? And what his soul desireth, that will he do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he will perform [what] is appointed for me; and many such things are with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore am I troubled at his presence; I consider, and I am afraid of him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ForGod hath made my heart soft, and the Almighty troubleth me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because I was not cut off before the darkness, neither hath he hidden the gloom from me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why are not times treasured up with the Almighty? why do not they that know him see his days?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They remove the landmarks; they violently take away the flocks and pasture them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They drive away the ass of the fatherless, they take the widow's ox for a pledge;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They turn the needy out of the way: the afflicted of the land all hide themselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lo, [as] wild asses in the desert, they go forth to their work, seeking early for the prey: the wilderness [yieldeth] them food for [their] children.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They reap in the field the fodder thereof, and they gather the vintage of the wicked;

translations.name: Darby Bible
