translations.text: They pass the night naked without clothing, and have no covering in the cold;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They are wet with the showers of the mountains, and for want of a shelter embrace the rock...

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They pluck the fatherless from the breast, and take a pledge of the poor:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These go naked without clothing, and, hungry, they bear the sheaf;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They press out oil within their walls, they tread their winepresses, and suffer thirst.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Men groan from out of the city, and the soul of the wounded crieth out; andGod imputeth not the impiety.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There are those that rebel against the light; they know not the ways thereof, nor abide in the paths thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The murderer riseth with the light, killeth the afflicted and needy, and in the night is as a thief.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the eye of the adulterer waiteth for the twilight, saying, No eye shall see me; and he putteth a covering on [his] face.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the dark they dig through houses; by day they shut themselves in; they know not the light:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the morning is to them all [as] the shadow of death; for they are familiar with the terrors of the shadow of death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He is swift on the face of the waters; their portion is cursed on the earth: he turneth not unto the way of the vineyards.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Drought and heat consume snow waters; so doth Sheol those that have sinned.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The womb forgetteth him; the worm feedeth sweetly on him: he shall be no more remembered; and unrighteousness is broken as a tree, —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that despoileth the barren that beareth not, and doeth not good to the widow:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He draweth also the mighty with his power; he riseth up, and no [man] is sure of life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [God] setteth him in safety, and he resteth thereon; but his eyes are upon their ways.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They are exalted for a little, and are no more; they are laid low; like all [other] are they gathered, and are cut off as the tops of the ears of corn.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If it be not so now, who will make me a liar, and make my speech nothing worth?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Bildad the Shuhite answered and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Dominion and fear are with him; he maketh peace in his high places.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is there any number of his troops? and upon whom doth not his light arise?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And how should man be just withGod? Or how should he be clean that is born of a woman?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lo, even the moon is not bright; and the stars are not pure in his sight:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How much less man, a worm, and the son of man, a worm!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Job answered and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How hast thou helped the powerless; how saved the arm that is without strength!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How hast thou counselled him that hath no wisdom, and abundantly declared the thing as it is!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For whom hast thou uttered words? and whose spirit came from thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The shades tremble beneath the waters and the inhabitants thereof;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sheol is naked before him, and destruction hath no covering.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He stretcheth out the north over empty space, he hangeth the earth upon nothing;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He bindeth up the waters in his thick clouds, and the cloud is not rent under them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He covereth the face of his throne, he spreadeth his cloud upon it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath traced a fixed circle over the waters, unto the confines of light and darkness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The pillars of the heavens tremble and are astonished at his rebuke.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He stirreth up the sea by his power, and by his understanding he smiteth through Rahab.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By his Spirit the heavens are adorned; his hand hath formed the fleeing serpent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lo, these are the borders of his ways; but what a whisper of a word do we hear of him! And the thunder of his power, who can understand?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Job continued his parable and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [As]God liveth, who hath taken away my right, and the Almighty, who hath embittered my soul,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the while my breath is in me, and the spirit ofGod is in my nostrils,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My lips shall not speak unrighteousness, nor my tongue utter deceit!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be it far from me that I should justify you; till I die I will not remove my blamelessness from me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My righteousness I hold fast, and will not let it go: my heart reproacheth [me] not one of my days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let mine enemy be as the wicked, and he that riseth up against me as the unrighteous.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For what is the hope of the ungodly, when [God] cutteth him off, whenGod taketh away his soul?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: WillGod hear his cry when distress cometh upon him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Doth he delight himself in the Almighty? will he at all times call uponGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will teach you concerning the hand ofGod; what is with the Almighty will I not conceal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, ye yourselves have all seen [it]; and why are ye thus altogether vain?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is the portion of the wicked man withGod, and the heritage of the violent, which they receive from the Almighty: —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If his children be multiplied, it is for the sword, and his offspring shall not be satisfied with bread;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Those that remain of him shall be buried by death, and his widows shall not weep.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Though he heap up silver as the dust, and prepare clothing as the clay;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He may prepare it, but the just shall put it on; and the innocent shall divide the silver.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He buildeth his house as the moth, and as a booth that a keeper maketh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He lieth down rich, but will do so no more; he openeth his eyes, and he is not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Terrors overtake him like waters; a whirlwind stealeth him away in the night.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The east wind carrieth him away and he is gone; and as a storm it hurleth him out of his place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [God] shall cast upon him and not spare: he would fain flee out of his hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Men] shall clap their hands at him, and shall hiss him out of his place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Surely there is a vein for the silver, and a place for gold which they refine;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Iron is taken out of the dust, and copper is molten out of the stone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Man] putteth an end to the darkness, and exploreth to the utmost limit, the stones of darkness and of the shadow of death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He openeth a shaft far from the inhabitants [of the earth]: forgotten of the foot, they hang suspended; away below men they hover.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As for the earth, out of it cometh bread, and underneath it is turned up as by fire;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The stones of it are the place of sapphires, and it hath dust of gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is a path no bird of prey knoweth, and the vulture's eye hath not seen it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The proud beasts have not trodden it, nor the fierce lion passed over it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Man] putteth forth his hand upon the flinty rock, he overturneth the mountains by the root.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He cutteth out channels in the rocks, and his eye seeth every precious thing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He bindeth the streams that they drip not, and what is hidden he bringeth forth to light.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But wisdom, where shall it be found? and where is the place of understanding?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Man knoweth not the value thereof; and it is not found in the land of the living.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The deep saith, It is not in me; and the sea saith, It is not with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Choice gold cannot be given for it, nor silver be weighed for its price.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is not set in the balance with gold of Ophir, with the precious onyx, and the sapphire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Gold and glass cannot be compared to it, nor vessels of fine gold be its exchange.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Corals and crystal are no more remembered; yea, the acquisition of wisdom is above rubies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The topaz of Ethiopia shall not be compared to it, neither shall it be set in the balance with pure gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whence then cometh wisdom? and where is the place of understanding?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For it is hidden from the eyes of all living, and concealed from the fowl of the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Destruction and death say, We have heard its report with our ears.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God understandeth the way thereof, and he knoweth its place:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he looketh to the ends of the earth, he seeth under the whole heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In making a weight for the wind, and meting out the waters by measure,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In appointing a statute for the rain, and a way for the thunder's flash:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then did he see it, and declare it; he established it, yea, and searched it out;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And unto man he said, Lo, the fear of the Lord, that is wisdom; and to depart from evil is understanding.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Job continued his parable and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Oh that I were as in months past, as in the days whenGod preserved me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When his lamp shone over my head, [and] by his light I walked through darkness;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As I was in the days of my youth, when the secret counsel ofGod was over my tent,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When the Almighty was yet with me, my young men round about me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When my steps were bathed in milk, and the rock poured out beside me rivers of oil! ...

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When I went out to the gate by the city, when I prepared my seat on the broadway,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The young men saw me, and hid themselves; and the aged arose [and] stood up;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Princes refrained from talking, and laid the hand on their mouth;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The voice of the nobles was hushed, and their tongue cleaved to their palate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When the ear heard [me], then it blessed me, and when the eye saw [me], it gave witness to me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I delivered the afflicted that cried, and the fatherless who had no helper.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The blessing of him that was perishing came upon me, and I caused the widow's heart to sing for joy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I put on righteousness, and it clothed me; my justice was as a mantle and a turban.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I was eyes to the blind, and feet was I to the lame;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I was a father to the needy, and the cause which I knew not I searched out;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I broke the jaws of the unrighteous, and plucked the spoil out of his teeth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I said, I shall die in my nest, and multiply my days as the sand;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My root shall be spread out to the waters, and the dew will lie all night on my branch;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My glory shall be fresh in me, and my bow be renewed in my hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Unto me they listened, and waited, and kept silence for my counsel:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: After my words they spoke not again, and my speech dropped upon them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they waited for me as for the rain, and they opened their mouth wide as for the latter rain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [If] I laughed on them, they believed [it] not; and they troubled not the serenity of my countenance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I chose their way, and sat as chief, and dwelt as a king in the army, as one that comforteth mourners.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But now they that are younger than I have me in derision, whose fathers I would have disdained to set with the dogs of my flock.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, whereto [should] the strength of their hands [profit] me, [men] in whom vigour hath perished?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Withered up through want and hunger, they flee into waste places long since desolate and desert:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They gather the salt-wort among the bushes, and the roots of the broom for their food.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They are driven forth from among [men] — they cry after them as after a thief —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To dwell in gloomy gorges, in caves of the earth and the rocks:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They bray among the bushes; under the brambles they are gathered together:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Sons of fools, and sons of nameless sires, they are driven out of the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now I am their song, yea, I am their byword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They abhor me, they stand aloof from me, yea, they spare not to spit in my face.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he hath loosed my cord and afflicted me; so they cast off the bridle before me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At [my] right hand rise the young brood; they push away my feet, and raise up against me their pernicious ways;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They mar my path, they set forward my calamity, without any to help them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They come in as through a wide breach: amid the confusion they roll themselves onward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Terrors are turned against me; they pursue mine honour as the wind; and my welfare is passed away like a cloud.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now my soul is poured out in me; days of affliction have taken hold upon me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The night pierceth through my bones [and detacheth them] from me, and my gnawing pains take no rest:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By their great force they have become my raiment; they bind me about as the collar of my coat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath cast me into the mire, and I have become like dust and ashes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I cry unto thee, and thou answerest me not; I stand up, and thou lookest at me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou art changed to a cruel one to me; with the strength of thy hand thou pursuest me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou liftest me up to the wind; thou causest me to be borne away, and dissolvest my substance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I know that thou wilt bring me to death, and into the house of assemblage for all living.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Indeed, no prayer [availeth] when he stretcheth out [his] hand: though they cry when he destroyeth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Did not I weep for him whose days were hard? was not my soul grieved for the needy?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I expected good, and there came evil; and I waited for light, but there came darkness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My bowels well up, and rest not; days of affliction have confronted me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I go about blackened, but not by the sun; I stand up, I cry in the congregation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am become a brother to jackals, and a companion of ostriches.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My skin is become black [and falleth] off me, and my bones are parched with heat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My harp also is [turned] to mourning, and my pipe into the voice of weepers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I made a covenant with mine eyes; and how should I fix my regard upon a maid?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For what would have been [my] portion ofGod from above, and what the heritage of the Almighty from on high?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is not calamity for the unrighteous? and misfortune for the workers of iniquity?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Doth not he see my ways, and number all my steps?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I have walked with falsehood, and my foot hath hasted to deceit,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (Let me be weighed in an even balance, andGod will take knowledge of my blamelessness;)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If my step have turned out of the way, and my heart followed mine eyes, and if any blot cleaveth to my hands;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let me sow, and another eat; and let mine offspring be rooted out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If my heart have been enticed unto a woman, so that I laid wait at my neighbour's door,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let my wife grind for another, and let others bow down upon her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this is an infamy; yea, it is an iniquity [to be judged by] the judges:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For it is a fire that consumeth to destruction, and would root out all mine increase.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I have despised the cause of my bondman or of my bondmaid, when they contended with me,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What then should I do whenGod riseth up? and if he visited, what should I answer him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Did not he that made me in the womb make him? and did not One fashion us in the womb?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I have withheld the poor from [their] desire, or caused the eyes of the widow to fail;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Or have eaten my morsel alone, so that the fatherless ate not thereof,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (For from my youth he grew up with me as with a father, and I have guided the [widow] from my mother's womb;)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I have seen any perishing for want of clothing, or any needy without covering;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If his loins have not blessed me, and if he were not warmed with the fleece of my lambs;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I have lifted up my hand against an orphan, because I saw my help in the gate:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Then] let my shoulder fall from the shoulder-blade, and mine arm be broken from the bone!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For calamity fromGod was a terror to me, and by reason of his excellency I was powerless.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I have made gold my hope, or said to the fine gold, My confidence!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I rejoiced because my wealth was great, and because my hand had gotten much;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I beheld the sun when it shone, or the moon walking in brightness,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And my heart have been secretly enticed, so that my mouth kissed my hand:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This also would be an iniquity for the judge, for I should have denied theGod who is above.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I rejoiced at the destruction of him that hated me, and exulted when evil befell him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (Neither have I suffered my mouth to sin by asking his life with a curse;)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If the men of my tent said not, Who shall find one that hath not been satisfied with his meat? —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The stranger did not lodge without; I opened my doors to the pathway.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I covered my transgressions as Adam, by hiding mine iniquity in my bosom,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because I feared the great multitude, and the contempt of families terrified me, so that I kept silence, and went not out of the door, ...

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Oh that I had one to hear me! Behold my signature: let the Almighty answer me! And let mine opponent write an accusation!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Would I not take it upon my shoulder? I would bind it on to me [as] a crown;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I would declare unto him the number of my steps; as a prince would I come near to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If my land cry out against me, and its furrows weep together;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If I have eaten the fruits thereof without money, and have tormented to death the souls of its owners:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let thistles grow instead of wheat, and tares instead of barley. The words of Job are ended.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these three men ceased to answer Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then was kindled the anger of Elihu the son of Barachel, the Buzite, of the family of Ram: against Job was his anger kindled, because he justified himself rather thanGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and against his three friends was his anger kindled, because they found no answer, and [yet] condemned Job.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Elihu had waited till Job had finished speaking, because they were older than he.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Elihu saw that there was no answer in the mouth of the three men, and his anger was kindled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite answered and said, I am young, and ye are aged; wherefore I was timid, and feared to shew you what I know.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I said, Let days speak, and multitude of years teach wisdom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But there is a spirit which is in man; and the breath of the Almighty giveth them understanding.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is not the great that are wise; neither do the aged understand judgment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore I say, Hearken to me; I also will shew what I know.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lo, I waited for your words; I gave ear to your reasonings, until ye searched out what to say.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, I gave you mine attention, and behold, there was none of you that confuted Job, that answered his words;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That ye may not say, We have found out wisdom;God will make him yield, not man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now he hath not directed [his] words against me; and I will not answer him with your speeches. ...

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They were amazed, they answered no more; words failed them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I waited, for they spoke not, but stood still, and answered no more; —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will answer, I also in my turn, I also will shew what I know:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I am full of matter, the spirit within me constraineth me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, my belly is as wine which hath no vent; like new flasks, it is ready to burst.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will speak, that I may find relief; I will open my lips and answer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let me not, I pray you, accept any man's person; neither will I give flattery to man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I know not how to flatter; my Maker would soon take me away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Howbeit, Job, I pray thee, hear mine utterances, and hearken to all my words.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold now, I have opened my mouth, my tongue speaketh in my palate,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My words shall be of the uprightness of my heart, and my lips shall utter knowledge purely.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Spirit ofGod hath made me, and the breath of the Almighty hath given me life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thou canst, answer me; array [thy words] before me: take thy stand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, beforeGod I am as thou; I also am formed out of the clay.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, my terror shall not make thee afraid, nor my burden be heavy upon thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Surely thou hast spoken in my hearing, and I have heard the voice of [thy] words: —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am clean without transgression; I am pure, and there is no iniquity in me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lo, he findeth occasions of hostility against me, he counteth me for his enemy;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He putteth my feet in the stocks, he marketh all my paths.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I will answer thee in this, thou art not right; forGod is greater than man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why dost thou strive against him? for he giveth not account of any of his matters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ForGod speaketh once, and twice, — [and man] perceiveth it not —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In a dream, in a vision of the night, when deep sleep falleth upon men, in slumberings upon the bed;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he openeth men's ears, and sealeth their instruction,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That he may withdraw man [from his] work, and hide pride from man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He keepeth back his soul from the pit, and his life from passing away by the sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He is chastened also with pain upon his bed, and with constant strife in his bones;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his life abhorreth bread, and his soul dainty food;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His flesh is consumed away from view, and his bones that were not seen stick out;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his soul draweth near to the pit, and his life to the destroyers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If there be a messenger with him, an interpreter, one among a thousand, to shew unto man his duty;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he will be gracious unto him, and say, Deliver him from going down to the pit: I have found a ransom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His flesh shall be fresher than in childhood; he shall return to the days of his youth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He shall pray untoGod, and he will receive him with favour; and he shall see his face with shoutings, and he will render unto man his righteousness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He will sing before men, and say, I have sinned, and perverted what was right, and it hath not been requited to me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath delivered my soul from going into the pit, and my life shall see the light.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lo, all these [things] workethGod twice, thrice, with man,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To bring back his soul from the pit, that he may be enlightened with the light of the living.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Mark well, Job, hearken unto me; be silent, and I will speak.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thou hast anything to say, answer me; speak, for I desire to justify thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If not, hearken thou unto me; be silent, and I will teach thee wisdom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Moreover Elihu answered and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear my words, ye wise [men]; and give ear unto me, ye that have knowledge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the ear trieth words, as the palate tasteth food.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let us choose for ourselves what is right; let us know among ourselves what is good!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Job hath said, I am righteous, andGod hath taken away my judgment:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Should I lie against my right? My wound is incurable without transgression.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What man is like Job? he drinketh up scorning like water,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And goeth in company with workers of iniquity, and walketh with wicked men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he hath said, It profiteth not a man if he delight himself inGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore hearken unto me, ye men of understanding: Far be wickedness fromGod, and wrong from the Almighty!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For a man's work will he render to him, and cause every one to find according to [his] way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yea, surely,God acteth not wickedly, and the Almighty perverteth not judgment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who hath entrusted to him the earth? and who hath disposed the whole world?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If he only thought of himself, [and] gathered unto him his spirit and his breath,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All flesh would expire together, and man would return to the dust.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If now [thou hast] understanding, hear this: give ear to the voice of my words!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Should he that hateth right indeed govern? and wilt thou condemn the All-just?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall one say to a king, Belial? to nobles, Wicked?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [How then to him] that accepteth not the persons of princes, nor regardeth the rich man more than the poor? for they are all the work of his hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In a moment they die, even at midnight the people are convulsed and pass away; and the strong are taken away without hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For his eyes are upon the ways of man, and he seeth all his steps.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is no darkness, nor shadow of death, where the workers of iniquity may hide themselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he doth not long consider a man, to bring him beforeGod in judgment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He breaketh in pieces mighty men without inquiry, and setteth others in their stead;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Since he knoweth their actions; and he overthroweth [them] in the night, and they are crushed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He striketh them as wicked men in the open sight of others,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because they have turned back from him, and would consider none of his ways;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So that they cause the cry of the poor to come unto him, and he heareth the cry of the afflicted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When he giveth quietness, who then will disturb? and when he hideth [his] face, who shall behold him? and this towards a nation, or towards a man alike;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That the ungodly man reign not, that the people be not ensnared.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For hath he said untoGod, I bear [chastisement], I will not offend;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What I see not, teach thou me; if I have done wrong, I will do so no more?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall he recompense according to thy mind? for thou hast refused [his judgment]; for thou so choosest, and not I; speak then what thou knowest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Men of understanding will say to me, and a wise man who heareth me:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Job hath spoken without knowledge, and his words were not with intelligence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Would that Job may be tried unto the end, because of [his] answers after the manner of evil men!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he addeth rebellion unto his sin, he clappeth [his hands] among us, and multiplieth his words againstGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Moreover Elihu answered and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thinkest thou this to be right, that thou saidst, My righteousness is more thanGod's?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou hast asked of what profit it is unto thee: what do I gain more than if I had sinned?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will reply to thee in words, and to thy companions with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Look unto the heavens and see; and survey the skies: they are higher than thou.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thou sinnest, what doest thou against him? If thy transgressions be multiplied, what doest thou unto him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thou be righteous, what givest thou to him? or what doth he receive of thy hand?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy wickedness [may affect] a man as thou [art], and thy righteousness a son of man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By reason of the multitude of oppressions they cry; they cry out by reason of the arm of the mighty:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But none saith, Where isGod my Maker, who giveth songs in the night,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who teacheth us more than the beasts of the earth, and maketh us wiser than the fowl of the heavens?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There they cry, and he answereth not, because of the pride of evil men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: SurelyGod will not hear vanity, neither will the Almighty regard it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Although thou sayest thou dost not see him, judgment is before him, therefore wait for him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But now, because he hath not visited in his anger, doth not [Job] know [his] great arrogancy?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Job hath opened his mouth in vanity, and made words abundant without knowledge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Elihu proceeded and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Suffer me a little, and I will shew thee that I have yet words forGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will fetch my knowledge from afar, and will ascribe righteousness to my Creator.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For truly my words shall be no falsehood: one perfect in knowledge is with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lo,God is mighty, but despiseth not [any]; mighty in strength of understanding:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He saveth not the wicked alive; but he doeth justice to the afflicted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He withdraweth not his eyes from the righteous, but with kings on the throne doth he even set them for ever; and they are exalted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if, bound in fetters, they be held in cords of affliction,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he sheweth them their work, and their transgressions, because they have increased.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he openeth their ear to discipline, and commandeth that they return from iniquity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If they hearken and serve [him], they shall accomplish their days in prosperity, and their years in pleasures.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if they hearken not, they shall pass away by the sword, and expire without knowledge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thegodless in heart heap up anger; they cry not when he bindeth them:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their soul dieth in youth, and their life is among the unclean.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he delivereth the afflicted in his affliction, and openeth their ear in [their] oppression.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Even so would he have allured thee out of the jaws of distress into a broad place, where there is no straitness; and the supply of thy table [would be] full of fatness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But thou art full of the judgments of the wicked: judgment and justice take hold [on thee].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because there is wrath, [beware] lest it take thee away through chastisement: then a great ransom could not avail thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Will he esteem thy riches? Not gold, nor all the resources of strength!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Desire not the night, when peoples are cut off from their place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take heed, turn not to iniquity; for this hast thou chosen rather than affliction.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lo,God is exalted in his power: who teacheth as he?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who hath appointed him his way? or who hath said, Thou hast wrought unrighteousness?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember that thou magnify his work, which men celebrate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All men look at it; man beholdeth [it] afar off.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lo,God is great, and we comprehend [him] not, neither can the number of his years be searched out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he draweth up the drops of water: they distil in rain from the vapour which he formeth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Which the skies pour down [and] drop upon man abundantly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But can any understand the spreadings of the clouds, [or] the crashing of his pavilion?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lo, he spreadeth his light around him, and covereth the bottom of the sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For with them he judgeth the peoples; he giveth food in abundance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [His] hands he covereth with lightning, and commandeth it where it is to strike.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His thundering declareth concerning him; the cattle even, concerning its coming.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Aye, my heart trembleth at this also, and leapeth up out of its place:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear attentively the roar of his voice, and the murmur going forth from his mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He sendeth it forth under the whole heaven, and his lightning unto the ends of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: After it a voice roareth: he thundereth with the voice of his excellency, and holdeth not back the flashes when his voice is heard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God thundereth marvellously with his voice, doing great things which we do not comprehend.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he saith to the snow, Fall on the earth! and to the pouring rain, even the pouring rains of his might.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He sealeth up the hand of every man; that all men may know his work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the wild beast goeth into its lair, and they remain in their dens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From the chamber [of the south] cometh the whirlwind; and cold from the winds of the north.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By the breath ofGod ice is given; and the breadth of the waters is straitened.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Also with plentiful moisture he loadeth the thick clouds, his light dispels the cloud;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they are turned every way by his guidance, that they may do whatsoever he commandeth them upon the face of the circuit of the earth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whether he cause it to come as a rod, or for his land, or in mercy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hearken unto this, Job; stand still and discern the wondrous works ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Dost thou know howGod hath disposed them, and how he causeth the lightning of his cloud to flash?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Dost thou know about the balancings of the clouds, the wondrous works of him that is perfect in knowledge?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How thy garments become warm when he quieteth the earth by the south wind?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hast thou with him spread out the sky, firm, like a molten mirror?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Teach us what we shall say unto him! We cannot order [our words] by reason of darkness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall it be told him if I would speak? if a man [so] say, surely he shall be swallowed up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now [men] see not the light as it gleameth, it is [hidden] in the skies. But the wind passeth by and cleareth them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From the north cometh gold; withGod is terrible majesty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Almighty, we cannot find him out: excellent in power, and in judgment, and in abundance of justice, he doth not afflict.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Men do therefore fear him: he respecteth not any that are wise of heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah answered Job out of the whirlwind and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who is this that darkeneth counsel by words without knowledge?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Gird up now thy loins like a man; and I will demand of thee, and inform thou me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Where wast thou when I founded the earth? Declare, if thou hast understanding.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who set the measures thereof — if thou knowest? or who stretched a line upon it?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whereupon were the foundations thereof sunken? or who laid its corner-stone,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When the morning stars sang together, and all the sons ofGod shouted for joy?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And who shut up the sea with doors, when it burst forth, issuing out of the womb?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When I made the cloud its garment, and thick darkness a swaddling band for it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When I cut out for it my boundary, and set bars and doors,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And said, Hitherto shalt thou come and no further, and here shall thy proud waves be stayed?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hast thou since thy days commanded the morning? hast thou caused the dawn to know its place,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That it might take hold of the ends of the earth, and the wicked might be shaken out of it?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is changed like the signet-clay; and [all things] stand forth as in a garment:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And from the wicked their light is withholden, and the uplifted arm is broken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hast thou entered as far as the springs of the sea? and hast thou walked in the recesses of the deep?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Have the gates of death been revealed unto thee? and hast thou seen the gates of the shadow of death?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hath thine understanding compassed the breadths of the earth? Declare if thou knowest it all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Where is the way to where light dwelleth? and the darkness, where is its place,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: That thou shouldest take it to its bound, and that thou shouldest know the paths to its house?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou knowest, for thou wast then born, and the number of thy days is great!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hast thou entered into the storehouses of the snow, and hast thou seen the treasuries of the hail,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Which I have reserved for the time of distress, for the day of battle and war?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By what way is the light parted, [and] the east wind scattered upon the earth?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who hath divided a channel for the rain-flood, and a way for the thunder's flash;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To cause it to rain on the earth, where no one is; on the wilderness wherein there is not a man;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To satisfy the desolate and waste [ground], and to cause the sprout of the grass to spring forth?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hath the rain a father? or who begetteth the drops of dew?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Out of whose womb cometh the ice? and the hoary frost of heaven, who bringeth it forth?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When the waters lie hidden as in stone, and the face of the deep holdeth fast together.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Canst thou fasten the bands of the Pleiades, or loosen the cords of Orion?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Dost thou bring forth the constellations each in its season? or dost thou guide the Bear with her sons?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Knowest thou the ordinances of the heavens? dost thou determine their rule over the earth?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Dost thou lift up thy voice to the clouds, that floods of waters may cover thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Dost thou send forth lightnings that they may go, and say unto thee, Here we are?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who hath put wisdom in the inward parts? or who hath given understanding to the mind?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who numbereth the clouds with wisdom? or who poureth out the bottles of the heavens,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When the dust runneth as into a molten mass, and the clods cleave fast together?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Dost thou hunt the prey for the lioness, and dost thou satisfy the appetite of the young lions,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When they crouch in [their] dens, [and] abide in the thicket to lie in wait?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who provideth for the raven his food, when his young ones cry untoGod, [and] they wander for lack of meat?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Knowest thou the time when the wild goats of the rock bring forth? dost thou mark the calving of the hinds?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Dost thou number the months that they fulfil? and knowest thou the time when they bring forth?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They bow themselves, they give birth to their young ones, they cast out their pains;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their young ones become strong, they grow up in the open field, they go forth, and return not unto them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who hath sent out the wild ass free? and who hath loosed the bands of the onager,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whose house I made the wilderness, and the salt plain his dwellings?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He laugheth at the tumult of the city, and heareth not the shouts of the driver;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The range of the mountains is his pasture, and he searcheth after every green thing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Will the buffalo be willing to serve thee, or will he lodge by thy crib?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Canst thou bind the buffalo with his cord in the furrow? or will he harrow the valleys after thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wilt thou put confidence in him, because his strength is great? and wilt thou leave thy labour to him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wilt thou trust him to bring home thy seed, and gather it into thy threshing-floor?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The wing of the ostrich beats joyously — But is it the stork's pinion and plumage?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For she leaveth her eggs to the earth, and warmeth them in the dust,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And forgetteth that the foot may crush them, or that the beast of the field may trample them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She is hardened against her young ones, as though they were not hers; her labour is in vain, without her concern.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ForGod hath deprived her of wisdom, and hath not furnished her with understanding.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What time she lasheth herself on high, she scorneth the horse and his rider.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hast thou given strength to the horse? hast thou clothed his neck with the quivering mane?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Dost thou make him to leap as a locust? His majestic snorting is terrible.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He paweth in the valley, and rejoiceth in [his] strength; he goeth forth to meet the armed host.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He laugheth at fear, and is not affrighted; neither turneth he back from before the sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The quiver rattleth upon him, the glittering spear and the javelin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He swalloweth the ground with fierceness and rage, and cannot contain himself at the sound of the trumpet:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At the noise of the trumpets he saith, Aha! and he smelleth the battle afar off, the thunder of the captains, and the shouting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Doth the hawk fly by thine intelligence, [and] stretch his wings toward the south?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Doth the eagle mount up at thy command, and make his nest on high?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He inhabiteth the rock and maketh his dwelling on the point of the cliff, and the fastness:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From thence he spieth out the prey, his eyes look into the distance;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his young ones suck up blood; and where the slain are, there is he.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah answered Job and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall he that will contend with the Almighty instruct [him]? he that reprovethGod, let him answer it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Job answered Jehovah and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I am nought: what shall I answer thee? I will lay my hand upon my mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Once have I spoken, and I will not answer; yea twice, but I will proceed no further.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah answered Job out of the whirlwind and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Gird up now thy loins like a man: I will demand of thee, and inform thou me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wilt thou also annul my judgment? wilt thou condemn me that thou mayest be righteous?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hast thou an arm likeGod? or canst thou thunder with a voice like him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Deck thyself now with glory and excellency, and clothe thyself with majesty and splendour.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Cast abroad the ragings of thine anger, and look on every one that is proud, and abase him:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Look on every one that is proud, bring him low, and tread down the wicked in their place:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hide them in the dust together; bind their faces in secret.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then will I also praise thee, because thy right hand saveth thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: See now the behemoth, which I made with thee: he eateth grass as an ox.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold now, his strength is in his loins, and his force is in the muscles of his belly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He bendeth his tail like a cedar; the sinews of his thighs are woven together.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His bones are tubes of bronze, his members are like bars of iron.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He is the chief ofGod's ways: he that made him gave him his sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the mountains bring him forth food, where all the beasts of the field play.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He lieth under lotus-bushes, in the covert of the reed and fen:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lotus-bushes cover him with their shade; the willows of the brook surround him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lo, the river overfloweth — he startleth not: he is confident though a Jordan break forth against his mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall he be taken in front? will they pierce through [his] nose in the trap?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wilt thou draw out the leviathan with the hook, and press down his tongue with a cord?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wilt thou put a rush-rope into his nose, and pierce his jaw with a spike?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Will he make many supplications unto thee? or will he speak softly unto thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Will he make a covenant with thee? wilt thou take him as a bondman for ever?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wilt thou play with him as with a bird, and wilt thou bind him for thy maidens?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shall partners make traffic of him, will they divide him among merchants?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wilt thou fill his skin with darts, and his head with fish-spears?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lay thy hand upon him; remember the battle, — do no more!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lo, hope as to him is belied: is not one cast down even at the sight of him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: None is so bold as to stir him up; and who is he that will stand before me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who hath first given to me, that I should repay [him]? [Whatsoever is] under the whole heaven is mine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I will not be silent as to his parts, the story of his power, and the beauty of his structure.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who can uncover the surface of his garment? who can come within his double jaws?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who can open the doors of his face? Round about his teeth is terror.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The rows of his shields are a pride, shut up together [as with] a close seal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: One is so near to another that no air can come between them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They are joined each to its fellow; they stick together, and cannot be sundered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His sneezings flash light, and his eyes are like the eyelids of the morning.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Out of his mouth go forth flames; sparks of fire leap out:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Out of his nostrils goeth smoke, as out of a boiling pot and cauldron.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His breath kindleth coals, and a flame goeth out of his mouth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In his neck lodgeth strength, and terror danceth before him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The flakes of his flesh are joined together: they are fused upon him, they cannot be moved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His heart is firm as a stone, yea, firm as the nether [millstone].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When he raiseth himself up, the mighty are afraid: they are beside themselves with consternation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If any reach him with a sword, it cannot hold; neither spear, nor dart, nor harpoon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He esteemeth iron as straw, bronze as rotten wood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The arrow will not make him flee; slingstones are turned with him into stubble.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Clubs are counted as stubble; he laugheth at the shaking of a javelin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: His under parts are sharp potsherds: he spreadeth a threshing-sledge upon the mire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He maketh the deep to boil like a pot; he maketh the sea like a pot of ointment;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He maketh the path to shine after him: one would think the deep to be hoary.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Upon earth there is not his like, who is made without fear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He beholdeth all high things; he is king over all the proud beasts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Job answered Jehovah and said,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I know that thou canst do everything, and that thou canst be hindered in no thought of thine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who is he that obscureth counsel without knowledge? therefore have I uttered what I did not understand; things too wonderful for me, which I knew not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hear, I beseech thee, and I will speak: I will demand of thee, and inform me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I had heard of thee by the hearing of the ear, but now mine eye seeth thee:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore I abhor [myself], and repent in dust and ashes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass after Jehovah had spoken these words to Job, that Jehovah said to Eliphaz the Temanite, Mine anger is kindled against thee, and against thy two friends; for ye have not spoken rightly of me, like my servant Job.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, take for yourselves seven bullocks and seven rams, and go to my servant Job, and offer up for yourselves a burnt-offering; and my servant Job shall pray for you, for him will I accept: lest I deal with you [after your] folly, for ye have not spoken of me rightly, like my servant Job.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Eliphaz the Temanite, and Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite, went and did according as Jehovah had said unto them; and Jehovah accepted Job.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah turned the captivity of Job, when he had prayed for his friends; and Jehovah gave Job twice as much as he had before.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all his brethren, and all his sisters, and all they that had been of his acquaintance before, came to him, and they ate bread with him in his house, and they condoled with him, and comforted him concerning all the evil that Jehovah had brought upon him; and every one gave him a piece of money, and every one a golden ring.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah blessed the latter end of Job more than his beginning; and he had fourteen thousand sheep, and six thousand camels, and a thousand yoke of oxen, and a thousand she-asses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he had seven sons and three daughters.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he called the name of the first, Jemimah; and the name of the second, Keziah; and the name of the third, Keren-happuch.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in all the land were no women found [so] fair as the daughters of Job; and their father gave them inheritance among their brethren.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Job lived after this a hundred and forty years, and saw his sons, and his sons' sons, four generations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Job died, old and full of days.
